name: Presto Lewiston Employees
description_short: Member-owned credit union serving Presto Products plant employees and their families in Lewiston, Utah since 1977, offering basic financial services.
description_long:
Presto Lewiston Employees Credit Union was established in 1977 to serve a specific employee population: workers at the Presto Products manufacturing plant in Lewiston, Utah, along with their family members. As a credit union, it operates on a member-owned, not-for-profit cooperative model rather than as a traditional for-profit bank. The organization has maintained a local presence in the Lewiston community for nearly five decades, focusing on serving its defined membership base.

The credit union offers standard banking services typical of small, community-focused financial institutions. Based on the available information, members can access financial products and services through the physical branch location. The limited hours of operation (Tuesday and Thursday, 9:30 a.m. to 11:30 a.m.) suggest a modest operation designed primarily for its member base rather than the general public. Members can contact the institution by phone at (435) 716-8588 for account inquiries and financial services.

What distinguishes Presto Lewiston Employees Credit Union is its exclusive focus on a tight-knit employee community rather than serving the general public. This specialization means members benefit from understanding the specific financial needs and circumstances of Presto Products employees. The long tenure since 1977 demonstrates stability and commitment to this particular membership base. As a member-owned cooperative, the credit union structure theoretically prioritizes member interests over shareholder profits.

Potential members should note that membership eligibility is restricted to Presto Products employees and their family members—this is not a bank open to the general public. The severely limited operating hours (only 4 hours per week) reflect its small-scale operation and may present accessibility challenges for members with inflexible work schedules. Prospective members should contact the institution directly to understand the full range of services offered and current membership requirements, as the public information available is minimal. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Member deposit accounts (checking and/or savings accounts based on standard credit union offerings)
In-person member services at branch location
pros: Member-owned cooperative structure prioritizes member interests over shareholder profits
Exclusive focus on Presto Products employee community creates targeted service approach
Local physical branch location in Lewiston with direct phone contact
Restricted membership base creates tight-knit financial community
cons: Extremely limited operating hours: only Tuesday and Thursday 9:30 a.m. to 11:30 a.m. (4 hours per week)
Membership restricted to Presto Products employees and family members only—not open to general public
Very limited public information available about services, products, and fees
Small operation may have limited product offerings compared to larger financial institutions
Geographic constraint: only serves the Lewiston, Utah area
best_for: Current and retired Presto Products plant employees seeking member-owned banking
Immediate family members of Presto Products employees needing basic banking services
Lewiston, Utah residents employed at the Presto Products facility
Employees seeking alternative to traditional banks with profit-sharing member structures
diagnosis: Presto Lewiston Employees Credit Union is best for current and former Presto Products plant employees and their immediate family members seeking membership in a stable, locally-rooted financial cooperative. The primary caveat is that membership is restricted to this specific employee population and operating hours are extremely limited (4 hours per week), making it impractical for those with typical business-hour banking needs.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
